source: pdftotext -layout '/Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2024/2024ND235.pdf' shows [¶2] begins with 'In 2018, Hoff pled guilty to murder, a class AA felony. note: DB text has the opening sentence of [¶2] misplaced to a separate line before the [¶2] marker, rather than inside the paragraph. This is a clear transcription/ingestion defect where the paragraph was improperly split. The fix restores the paragraph marker to the correct position at the paragraph's beginning.

new_exact: did not show extreme circumstances sufficient to justify dismissal with prejudice. The court determined
source: PDF source /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2024/2024ND220.pdf lines 169-171
evidence: The DB text contains a scrambled phrase in paragraph 12 where 'circumstances sufficient to justify' is transposed to 'justify...circumstances sufficient to'. The correct word order is confirmed by the authoritative PDF source which reads: 'the case presented did not show extreme circumstances sufficient to justify dismissal with prejudice. The court determined dismissal without prejudice...' This is a clear OCR or text extraction error producing a grammatically incorrect and semantically corrupted sentence in the DB.
note: This is a critical content error that corrupts the meaning of a key finding in the opinion. The scrambled phrase breaks the logical flow of the court's analysis of appropriate sanctions.

source: PDF source (pdftotext): line 308, compact four-dot ellipsis form
evidence: ND opinions print the compact four-dot form '....' not spaced '. . . .' The DB shows spaced form '. . . . .' (9 characters: period space period space period space period space period) at the sole occurrence in paragraph 18 (Detective Satermo questioning). The authoritative PDF source at the same location shows the compact four-dot form '....' on its own line. This is a spacing artifact from the DB ingestion process that converted the PDF's displayed ellipsis into reflowed prose.
note: Single occurrence at position 16303 in DB, in the context: 'A. No. . . . . Q. And I did ask you this earlier...' This should become 'A. No. .... Q. And I did ask you this earlier...' The paragraph structure matches the PDF; only the ellipsis representation differs.
